meta_title: For Students: user onboarding to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: User Onboarding to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Struggling with project ideas? Learn how to build a professional user onboarding flow using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your first portfolio app.
content.answer_block: The 'no project ideas' trap is solved by focusing on utility over originality. Build a modular user onboarding system—a universal component every SaaS needs. By mastering state management, progress tracking, and interactive UI, you create a high-value portfolio piece that demonstrates technical maturity and immediate professional viability for recruiters.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the developer's greatest adversary. You have the skills, the stack, and the ambition, but the dreaded 'no project ideas' paralysis keeps your repository empty. You aren't failing; you are simply looking for the wrong kind of spark. Stop trying to invent the next unicorn and start building the infrastructure that every unicorn requires.

User onboarding is the heartbeat of modern software. It is a microcosm of full-stack engineering: database design, state transitions, UI/UX psychology, and edge-case handling. By building a robust, reusable onboarding module, you aren't just writing code; you are building a professional asset that proves to employers you understand how to guide a user from zero to value.

steps.title: Define the Onboarding Schema
steps.description: Establish a robust data structure to track user progress through onboarding stages. This ensures your app remembers exactly where a user left off.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a JSON schema and TypeScript interface for a multi-step user onboarding flow, including current step, completion status, and user metadata fields.

steps.title: Build the State Machine
steps.description: Implement a state machine to handle transitions between onboarding screens, preventing invalid navigation and ensuring data integrity.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a React hook using XState or a simple reducer pattern to manage onboarding transitions and validate step progression.

steps.title: Design Interactive UI Components
steps.description: Create modular UI components for each onboarding step, focusing on accessibility and responsive design principles.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a reusable StepProgressBar and a FormContainer component in Tailwind CSS for a modern, clean onboarding interface.

steps.title: Implement Progress Persistence
steps.description: Connect the frontend state to a backend database so user progress is saved across sessions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a secure API endpoint to update the user onboarding progress in a Supabase or PostgreSQL database.

steps.title: Test Edge Cases and Recovery
steps.description: Simulate network failures and interrupted sessions to ensure the onboarding flow remains resilient.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write unit tests for the onboarding state machine to ensure it handles interruptions and resumes correctly.

pro_tips: Always build onboarding as a separate micro-service or module so you can drop it into any future project.
Use 'optimistic UI' updates so the onboarding feels instant, even if the backend database is still processing.
Track onboarding abandonment points in your logs to identify which steps are too complex or confusing for users.
Implement a 'Skip' button with conditional logic to respect experienced users who want to jump straight to the product.
Add micro-animations to your progress bar to provide immediate dopamine hits, increasing user retention during the setup phase.
resources: State Machines in UI Design: https://stately.ai/docs
content.next_steps: Once the onboarding flow is complete, integrate it into your existing portfolio site and write a blog post detailing the 'State Machine' architecture you implemented to impress hiring managers.
content.conversion_block: Your portfolio is the bridge between being a student and being a hired engineer. Build this onboarding flow, polish the UI, and deploy it to demonstrate your capability to handle real-world user flows. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
